         
    0. A failing snyk-test will not stop web's pipeline.
       Only the sdlc-gate will see the failure.

    1. Add steps to write to github summary, eg from kosli-begin-trail
    - name: Write Trail URL to GitHub Step Summary
      shell: bash
      run: |
        url="https://app.kosli.com/${KOSLI_ORG}/flows/${KOSLI_FLOW}/trails/${KOSLI_TRAIL}"
        echo "[Kosli Trail](${url})" > "${GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY}"

    3. jq has ability to convert timestamp to/from iso8601 date string.

    4. You could add a snyk key for the age of the ignore entry (based on now)
       This could have a jq rule varying on severity.


combine_snyk.py 

      Add json entry for .snyk ignore entry that is for a vuln 
      that the artifact does not have.

aws-beta.yml
      
      1. Create env-policy for aws-beta and attach to aws-beta.
      2. Write tests for custom attestation types.

env_snyk_test.yml

      1. For release, remove use of fake-data
      - name: Generate JSON for each Artifact in KOSLI_ENV for following job's strategy:matrix:include
        id: set-artifacts
        run: |
          # FAKE getting snapshot from kosli_env so we only do 1 snyk-container-test
          artifacts="$(jq --compact-output . ./fake-data/artifacts-${{ inputs.kosli_env }}.json)"
          echo "artifacts=${artifacts}" >> ${GITHUB_OUTPUT}          

      2. For release, comment in id: of step using real data
      - name: Generate JSON for each Artifact in KOSLI_ENV for following job's strategy:matrix:include
        #id: set-artifacts
        run: |
          artifacts="$(make artifacts | jq --raw-output --compact-output .)"


artifact_snyk_test.yml

      0. git-checkout on line 76 is needed for bin/combine_snyk.py 
         But when called from web repo, this checks out web.

      1. Call artifact_snyk_test.yml instead of snyk job in main workflow of 
         all service repos.
         Should this attest to the build trail?
         The gate is not the trail compliance, it is the deployment gate to aws-beta.
         So it is reasonable that it deploys to a different trail.
         And that fits with the idea of fixing synk vulns being othogonal
         (as much as possible) to deploying to aws-beta.
         Issue: Is there going to be an OIDC problem?
         Yes. Need to make it a composite action.

      ?. Embed the date/time of the trail inside the json for each artifact
      TWICE, once as a string in human readable format, again as a number 
      in a form suitable for jq rule comparing with now.




-----------------------------------------------

    ?Add --annotate flags to custom-attestations so key values are easy to see.
      
      days_since_first_seen="$(jq 
        '(now - .first_seen_ts) / (24*60*60) | floor' 
        <<< '{"first_seen_ts":1772222992}'
      )"
      
      days_till_ignore_expires="$(jq
        '(.ignore_expires_ts - now) / (24*60*60) | floor'
        <<< '{"ignore_expires_ts:1772582992}'
      )"
